summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Left shoulder pain.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports progressive left shoulder pain over the past six weeks, exacerbated by overhead activities and lying on the affected side. He denies recent trauma. The patient states that acetaminophen provides partial relief. He also reports a fall while playing volleyball last night, landing on his arm after being kicked in the legs by another player. The patient was playing volleyball yesterday. He also reports a dirty ball last night.

Findings
Examination reveals limited active abduction of the left shoulder and pain with impingement testing. The rest of the patient's body is normal. The doctor observes a proximal humerus fracture. The patient can straighten their arm.

Assessment
The patient's presentation is most consistent with a proximal humerus fracture and rotator cuff tendinopathy.

Plan
The doctor will put the patient in a long arm cast and a sling. The doctor ordered a check in two weeks. The doctor prescribed lortab 500-5 mg tablets. The doctor prescribed 20 lortab tablets.
reference_summary:
CC:

Right upper arm pain.

HPI:

Ms. Russell is a 39-year-old female who presents today for an evaluation of severe right upper arm pain. She states she was playing volleyball yesterday and fell and landed on her arm. She has been taking ibuprofen every 6 hours but it does not help. She rates her pain 9/10. She denies any numbness or tingling in her arm. She has a history of gallstones and takes Pepcid. She has a past surgical history of a lumbar fusion 6 years ago. She denies any allergies.

EXAM

Examination of the right upper extremity shows swelling and erythema of the right shoulder. Tenderness over the right shoulder. Normal pulses.

RESULTS

X-rays of the right humerus, 2 views, obtained on today's visit show a proximal humerus fracture.

IMPRESSION

Right proximal humerus fracture.

PLAN

At this point, I discussed the diagnosis and treatment options with the patient. I have recommended we place her into a long arm cast and sling. She will follow up in 2 weeks for repeat imaging. At that point, we may need to discuss surigical options. I will prescribe Lortab, 5 mg, #20 (twenty) to take every 6 hours as needed for pain.

All questions were answered.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor observes a proximal humerus fracture.
section_miscategorizations.reason: A fracture diagnosis is a clinical impression/synthesis rather than a raw exam or test finding; placing it under Findings could mislead a reader into thinking this is purely an observed physical sign rather than the diagnostic assessment.
